subject: Support 7.2.1 VES in TCAGEN2
commit_message:
Support 7.2.1 VES in TCAGEN2

- Includes modifications for processing Tca Policy as a List
  to support both v5 and v7 policies

Issue-ID: DCAEGEN2-2976
Signed-off-by: Malarvizhi Paramasivam <malarvizhi.44@wipro.com>
Change-Id: I29f56048d825710d268e4588f4b5779d5fdb6020

Patch Set 3:

Hi, I found these errors in the Jenkins logs where its apparently searching for the ChangeLog.md in the individual modules, but its not present. May I know if I should add it in the individual modules too? 

<<< ERROR: (changelog-missing) No dcae-analytics/dcae-analytics-model/ChangeLog.md found >>>
<<< ERROR: (changelog-missing) No dcae-analytics/dcae-analytics-tca-web/ChangeLog.md found >>>
<<< ERROR: (changelog-missing) No dcae-analytics/dcae-analytics-test/ChangeLog.md found >>>
..
..

Patch Set 3:

> Patch Set 3:
> 
> Hi, I found these errors in the Jenkins logs where its apparently searching for the ChangeLog.md in the individual modules, but its not present. May I know if I should add it in the individual modules too? 
> 
> <<< ERROR: (changelog-missing) No dcae-analytics/dcae-analytics-model/ChangeLog.md found >>>
> <<< ERROR: (changelog-missing) No dcae-analytics/dcae-analytics-tca-web/ChangeLog.md found >>>
> <<< ERROR: (changelog-missing) No dcae-analytics/dcae-analytics-test/ChangeLog.md found >>>
> ..
> ..

Please add the following block to the LOWER-level pom.xml files, such as
dcae-analytics/dcae-analytics-model/pom.xml:

                  <properties>
                    <onap-gerrit-review>
                      -changelog-missing
                    </onap-gerrit-review>
                  </properties>

This will tell onap-gerrit-review that THIS project is not using sub-repo changelogs.

Thanks.

The next time you run upload a patch, the Jenkins log will include information on this.

Patch Set 3: Code-Review-1

(6 comments)

found a few more possibly-minor things

Patch Set 3: Code-Review-1

(2 comments)

Couple comments
1) This patch relies on Policy Configuration to contain both rules for v5 and v7 which may not be true always (as config can set to only v5 specific policy or v7 and not both). Code should support this case and in class where tca policy count==2 is checked - this should be replaced to use list.size
2) Upper limit for list is defined hence when policy list contains more than 2 entry, error should be reported as this is not expected.
3) Proposed TCA Policy configuration will impact the TCA policy model and possibly policy/xacml module.  Got these references from Jorge (Policy team)
   https://git.onap.org/policy/models/tree/models-examples/src/main/resources/policytypes/onap.policies.monitoring.tcagen2.yaml
  https://git.onap.org/policy/models/tree/models-examples/src/main/resources/policies/vFirewall.policy.monitoring.input.tosca.json
  These files should be updated to be consistent with what TCA application expects. 

Noticed as v5 and v7s model are sort of combined in processing and handled same, there could be a situation where tca's can be generated/cleared across different VES input, that is abatement event on v7 (coming with same sourcename) could clear event triggered from v5 onset. Chances of this occurring in network is minimal though so not an big issue.

The impact on policy/xacml is something not anticipated. I've requested Jorge to schedule a call to assess Policy team impact for this proposed model.

> Patch Set 3: Code-Review-1
> 
> (2 comments)
> 
> Couple comments
> 1) This patch relies on Policy Configuration to contain both rules for v5 and v7 which may not be true always (as config can set to only v5 specific policy or v7 and not both). Code should support this case and in class where tca policy count==2 is checked - this should be replaced to use list.size
> 2) Upper limit for list is defined hence when policy list contains more than 2 entry, error should be reported as this is not expected.
> 3) Proposed TCA Policy configuration will impact the TCA policy model and possibly policy/xacml module.  Got these references from Jorge (Policy team)
>    https://git.onap.org/policy/models/tree/models-examples/src/main/resources/policytypes/onap.policies.monitoring.tcagen2.yaml
>   https://git.onap.org/policy/models/tree/models-examples/src/main/resources/policies/vFirewall.policy.monitoring.input.tosca.json
>   These files should be updated to be consistent with what TCA application expects. 
> 
> Noticed as v5 and v7s model are sort of combined in processing and handled same, there could be a situation where tca's can be generated/cleared across different VES input, that is abatement event on v7 (coming with same sourcename) could clear event triggered from v5 onset. Chances of this occurring in network is minimal though so not an big issue.
> 
> The impact on policy/xacml is something not anticipated. I've requested Jorge to schedule a call to assess Policy team impact for this proposed model.

Hi Vijay, I regret for not clarifying this change beforehand, when I was testing the functionality with kubernetes cluster I found that the policy was fetched from the app-config only (only values.yaml) unlike the kpi-ms/son-handler where we load the policies into the policy pod. I've made a few of the changes from your comments. Will address the rest once I analyse the changes again.

A few more small things.

> > Couple comments

Code should support this case and in class where tca policy count==2 is checked - this should be replaced to use list.size
> > 2) Upper limit for list is defined hence when policy list contains more than 2 entry, error should be reported as this is not expected.
> > 3) Proposed TCA Policy configuration will impact the TCA policy model and possibly policy/xacml module.  Got these references from Jorge (Policy team)
> >    https://git.onap.org/policy/models/tree/models-examples/src/main/resources/policytypes/onap.policies.monitoring.tcagen2.yaml
> >   https://git.onap.org/policy/models/tree/models-examples/src/main/resources/policies/vFirewall.policy.monitoring.input.tosca.json
> >   These files should be updated to be consistent with what TCA application expects. 
> > 
> > Noticed as v5 and v7s model are sort of combined in processing and handled same, there could be a situation where tca's can be generated/cleared across different VES input, that is abatement event on v7 (coming with same sourcename) could clear event triggered from v5 onset. Chances of this occurring in network is minimal though so not an big issue.
> > 
> > The impact on policy/xacml is something not anticipated. I've requested Jorge to schedule a call to assess Policy team impact for this proposed model.
> 
> Hi Vijay, I regret for not clarifying this change beforehand, when I was testing the functionality with kubernetes cluster I found that the policy was fetched from the app-config only (only values.yaml) unlike the kpi-ms/son-handler where we load the policies into the policy pod. I've made a few of the changes from your comments. Will address the rest once I analyse the changes again.

No issues.. Discussion with policy team should happend next week, lets determine next step based on that. If concerns to support new model, we can look into alternate options (one case could be to retain policy model as-is and introduce new app-configuration/property for TCA to set processing for only v7 or v5 and not both. So when v7 is enabled tca.policy will be configured to use "domain":"measurement" and if the events are coming in v5 - they would be ignored). Anyways, lets decide this after the meeting.

Commented inline on few other findings
